title: 5 Image Files for Member Statements
body_text:
5	Image Files for Member Statements
5.1	Overview of Image Files for Member Statements
Except for Unqualified Items and Chargebacks, physical cheques are not available to credit unions or their members. Credit unions can provide Images of Incoming Clearing Items in their membersГўВҖВҷ statements if the credit union obtains Image Files from Central 1.
Provision of Image Files to the credit union or its statement service provider is optional. Each credit union can receive one Image File per month as part of its Clearing fee. Additional fees apply if more than one set of Image Files are requested (e.g., to multiple entities or locations) or if the Image File is required on a daily basis.
The following table describes which items are included in the Image File.
Items
Description
Included in Image File
Qualified Items
Qualified Items are drawn on the memberГўВҖВҷs account. Since these items meet CPA standards and are MICR encoded with the Transit Number and dollar value, they are included in ORS and the Image Files.
Yes
Dishonoured Items
These are items drawn on a memberГўВҖВҷs account that the credit union has dishonoured.
Monthly Image File
ГўВҖВ“ No
Daily Image File
ГўВҖВ“ No, if dishonoured before the file was created.
Qualified Items processed on the last day of the statement cycle
Qualified Items processed on the last day of the statement cycle are included in the Image File. For example, when the statement cycles on the 15th of the month, the Image File will include items from the 16th of the previous month to the 15th of the current month (inclusive).
Yes
Reinstated Cheques
If the credit union modifies the data associated with an Image to assign it a different account number, cheque number, or Transit Number, it is known as a reinstated cheque. Credit unions can modify this data if it was incorrectly captured or to reassign an item that changed due to mergers or account consolidations. Items that are reinstated with the correct information improve the match rate for Cheque Image Statements. Items can be reinstated on Central 1ГўВҖВҷs ORS application. Refer to the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures.
Provision of the original Image is optional
Unqualified Items
Unqualified Items are drawn on a memberГўВҖВҷs account, but they are too damaged for Central 1 to create an Image. The physical items are returned to the credit union with an adjustment slip. Since Unqualified Items are treated as adjustments, they are not included in the daily Posting File (for Ontario credit unions), the DREC/QREC File (for BC credit unions), ORS, or the Clearing reports (CLI, CLO, and CLUS). Refer to the Incoming Clearing Items Part for more information.
No
5.2	Requesting Image Files
Proceed as follows to request Image Files from Central 1:
Complete the Application for Image File Delivery, Form 1916. See Figure 1. Completion of this form is self-explanatory.
Figure 1: Application for Image File Delivery, Form 1916
Forward the completed form to Central 1 as directed in the instructions.
Central 1 will perform the required set-up to create Image Files and make them available to the designated organization (e.g., the credit union or the statement service provider). Image Files are generated according to the specifications indicated on the Application for Image File Delivery, Form 1916 (e.g., fronts only or fronts and backs).
Related Information
Application for Image File Delivery,
Form 1916
5.3	Obtaining Image Files
For the majority of credit unions, Central 1 provides the Image Files directly to the statement service provider. Central 1 will discuss connectivity and file size restrictions directly with the service provider.
Note:	The maximum file size for the VPN connection is 15,000 Images per file.
If Central 1 delivers the Image Files directly to the credit union, the credit union downloads the files from Central 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange. For BC credit unions, the Image Files (IMG files) are located in the credit unionГўВҖВҷs root directory. For Ontario credit unions, the Image Files (.TIF) are located in the ГўВҖВңimagefilesГўВҖВқ subfolder of the Clearing folder. Refer to the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for information on accessing the File and Report Exchange.
Note:	The maximum file size for Central 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange is 5,000 Images per file.
Image Files for most credit unions are produced once per month. For credit unions with a high volume of Incoming Clearing Items, a single file may be too large for processing. As a result, multiple Image Files will be created during the course of the month at intervals coordinated with the credit unionГўВҖВҷs statement cycles.
The cost of creating an Image File or set of Image Files (e.g., split files) is included in the per item cheque Clearing charge. Duplicate files are available for an extra charge. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725.
Related Information
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario)
END
